title: The Small Conjecture
statement_text: Every small hereditary class has bounded twin-width.
context_text: The paper proves that every bounded twin-width class is small (has at most $n! c^n$ labeled $n$-vertex graphs for some constant $c$), unifying results for bounded treewidth, proper subclasses of permutation graphs, and proper minor-closed classes. The small conjecture posits the converse. The authors explore it by verifying bounded twin-width for many natural small hereditary classes that could serve as counterexamples, including subdivisions of $K_n$, graphs of bounded stack or queue number, and cubic expanders from iterated random 2-lifts.
notes: Explicitly named 'the small conjecture' repeatedly in the abstract and introduction; no labelled theorem environment visible in the extracted text. The PDF extraction is truncated, so later numbered conjecture environments may exist.

title: Implicit Graph Conjecture
statement_text: Every factorial hereditary class (i.e., hereditary class with growth $n!^{O(1)}$) admits an $O(\log n)$-adjacency labeling scheme.
context_text: The implicit graph conjecture posits that any hereditary graph class with at most $n!^{O(1)}$ labeled $n$-vertex graphs has an adjacency labeling scheme using $O(\log n)$ bits per vertex. The paper obtains such a scheme for all bounded twin-width classes as a second consequence of the linear-arity contraction tree, confirming the conjecture for this broad family.
paper_contribution: Proves that every graph class of bounded twin-width admits an $O(\log n)$-adjacency labeling scheme, confirming the implicit graph conjecture for all bounded twin-width hereditary classes.
notes: No labelled theorem environment for the conjecture statement visible in the extracted text. Attribution to Kannan–Naor–Rudich 1992 is standard in the literature; the paper does not include an explicit citation in the visible portion of the extracted text.

title: Question: polynomial expansion and twin-width
statement_text: Do classes with polynomial expansion have bounded twin-width?
context_text: Towards the small conjecture, the authors identify showing that small sparse classes have bounded (sparse) twin-width as a first, challenging step. Since classes with polynomial expansion are small, confirming bounded twin-width for them would constitute meaningful progress.
notes: Stated as an explicit open question in the introduction without a labelled environment. PDF extraction is truncated; additional numbered questions may appear in later sections.
